name: W.P. Archery and Targeting
description: Expertise with thrown and bow weapons. It includes throwing spears, forks, slings, short bow, long bow, cross bow, and modern bows. Notes: The character loses all bonuses and rate of fire is half when riding on horse back or a moving vehicle.
stackingRule: Strike and parry bonuses from each tier are cumulative. Rate of fire increases as indicated.

name: W.P. Battle Axe
description: Training in all types of battle axes and picks.
stackingRule: Strike, parry, and throw bonuses from each tier are cumulative.

name: W.P. Blunt
description: Training with all types of blunt weapons, including maces, hammers, cudgels, pikes, staves, and clubs.
stackingRule: Strike and parry bonuses from each tier are cumulative.

name: W.P. Chain
description: Training with all types of chain weapons, including ordinary lengths of chain, nunchaku, flail, and ball or mace and chain.
stackingRule: Strike and parry bonuses from each tier are cumulative.

name: W.P. Knife
description: Training with all types of knives.
stackingRule: Strike, parry, and throw bonuses from each tier are cumulative.

name: W.P. Polearm
description: Training with the large spear-like weapons known as the polearm. Also includes tridents.
stackingRule: Strike, parry, and throw bonuses from each tier are cumulative.

name: W.P. Sword
description: Combat skills with large and small swords, including fencing type training. Includes rapiers, sabers, two handed, and short swords.
stackingRule: Strike and parry bonuses from each tier are cumulative.

name: W.P. Whip
description: Skill at whipping or snapping with long, flexible, light weapons. Notes: A typical whip and Cat-o-Nine tails inflicts 1D6 damage, a bull whip 2D4 damage.
stackingRule: Strike, damage, and entangle bonuses from each tier are cumulative.

name: W.P. Revolver
description: All cylinder style hand guns; not automatic (trigger must be pulled each time the gun is fired).

name: W.P. Automatic Pistol
description: All modern military automatic pistols (automatic means the weapon keeps firing while the trigger is depressed and until the trigger is released or the rounds are all expended).

name: W.P. Bolt-Action Rifle
description: Includes most hunting rifles; not an automatic firing weapon.

name: W.P. Automatic and Semi-automatic Rifles
description: Includes all assault rifles like the M-16 and AK-47.

name: W.P. Sub-Machinegun
description: Includes all small automatic weapons like the Uzi.

name: W.P. Heavy
description: Includes machineguns, bazookas, LAWS, and mortars.

name: W.P. Paired Weapons
description: A tactic in which a one-handed weapon is held in each hand (up to four) to be used in any combination of combat moves simultaneously. For example, two short swords, a short sword and broadsword, sword and knife, or mace and shield. Two-handed weapons cannot be used as paired weapons.

pairedWeapons.summary: Dual-wield tactic: one suitable one-handed weapon per hand (up to four arms). Negates off-hand penalties for weapons in which the character has the corresponding W.P. Without the required W.P. per weapon, or if a weapon is too large or heavy, paired use is impossible—the character can attack only one opponent and each swipe counts as one melee attack.
weaponCoverage: one-handed melee weapons with qualifying W.P.
pairedWeapons.notes: Roll twice when striking two different opponents, parrying attacks from two separate opponents, or striking with one weapon and parrying with the other (against one or two targets). One roll to strike or parry only when striking the same opponent twice or parrying two attacks from the same opponent. When using both attacks simultaneously on one foe, the character is vulnerable to a second opponent; against three or more attackers, may parry two but cannot parry other blows before the next available action. Four-armed characters take this W.P. once; taking it again grants no additional bonuses. Dual pistols are not the focus of this skill: -2 to strike with the regular hand and -6 with the off-hand.

specialManeuvers.name: Simultaneous Strike and Parry
specialManeuvers.description: Strike and parry simultaneously—two actions for every one melee action/attack. Requires available attacks to strike; a character who has used all attacks cannot counterstrike until the next melee round but may still parry.

specialManeuvers.name: Twin Strike
specialManeuvers.description: Twin, simultaneous strikes against the same target. Both weapons hit at once as ONE melee attack/action (roll only once to strike with both weapons). The defender can parry only one weapon unless they also have W.P. Paired Weapons and use two weapons, or a weapon and shield, to block both (one parry roll).

specialManeuvers.name: Strike Multiple Targets
specialManeuvers.description: Strike two different targets, or strike one and parry another incoming attack, simultaneously; both must be within reach.

specialManeuvers.name: Simultaneous Parries
specialManeuvers.description: Parry two different attackers, one with each hand.

name: W.P. Shotgun
description: A familiarity with the classic double-barrel shotgun and police and military versions of shotguns with ammo drums. Notes: Each single blast counts as one melee attack; police/military shotguns with ammo drums cannot fire two simultaneous blasts but feature rapid-fire and larger payloads.
stackingRule: Strike bonuses from each tier are cumulative.

specialManeuvers.name: Double Barrel Blast
specialManeuvers.description: Double the damage if both barrels are fired simultaneously.
